page.title: Eianji Higashi Kofun - Eianji Nishi Kofun

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Eianji Higashi Kofun - Eianji Nishi Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Eianji Higashi Kofun・Eianji Nishi Kofun | native_name =永安寺東古墳・永安寺西古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Eianji Higashi Kofun・Eianji Nishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|57|06|N|130|34|36|E|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|06|N|130|34|36|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}}
The {{nihongo|'''Eianji Higashi Kofun・Eianji Nishi Kofun'''|永安寺東古墳・永安寺西古墳||qq=}} are a pair [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tamana neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}. The tumuli were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139897|title= 永安寺東古墳・永安寺西古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Eianji kofun are located at the foot of a hill overlooking the alluvial plain on the right bank of the Kikuchi River in northern Kumamoto Prefecture. The site takes is name from the ruins of a Buddhist temple called Eian-ji, which is located about 40 meters away. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> They are thought to have been built between the end of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century. The Eianji Higashi Kofun is a [[decorated kofun]]. Although most of the mound has been lost, it is believed to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}-style circular tumulus with a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with an opening to the southeast. Only a portion of the antechamber and burial chamber proper remain. The main burial chamber is 2.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.7 meters high and was made of huge monoliths of processed lava from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|qid=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}} with a stone roof. The walls have triangular engraved lines, suggesting that it was painted. The antechamber is currently 1.6 meters long, 2.3 meters wide and 1.6 meters high, and shallow vertical lines have been carved into two stones on either side of the back wall, with continuous triangular patterns carved into the left and right sides and painted red. Circular patterns and patterns that appear to be boats and horses are also painted on the left and right side walls. <ref name="Shiseki"/> The Eianji Nishi Kofun is also thought to be a small circular tumulus, and the stone chamber is 3.4 meters long, 2.8 meters wide and 3 meters high, constructed on three sides from a single megalithic stone, with the stone roof of the back wall and parts of the [[fukiishi|paving stones]] remaining. It is also orientated to the south. Three rows of circular patterns are carved into the back wall and both side walls, and red paint remains in some places.<ref name="Shiseki"/> At Eianji Nishi Tomb, construction was carried out to cover the entire mound and stone chamber with a large dome-shaped roof for protection. At Eianji Higashi Tomb, the stone chamber has been partially restored and a protective structure made of reinforced concrete has been installed, and visitors can now tour the site. The site is about 1.4 kilometers northeast of {{ill|Shin-Tamana Station|en|Shin-Tamana Station|qid=Q1136695|lt=Shin-Tamana Station|1=Shin-Tamana Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|qid=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen|1=Kyushu Shinkansen}}. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.tamana.lg.jp/q/aview/331/16342.html Tamana City home page] Eianji Higashi Kofun - Eianji Nishi Kofun<
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
